analysis.test: Copyright infringement: (1) the claimed work must be original — bearing the imprint of the author's personality as expressed through creative choices […] (L112-1 CPI); (2) the original elements of form must have been reproduced or represented without authorization (L112-4 CPI). Ideas, themes, game mechanics, and generic formulations belong to the public domain and are not protectable. It is incumbent upon the party […] invoking protection to specifically identify the original elements and to demonstrate their reproduction in the accused work (Exhibit 12, TJ Paris 18/12/2020; Exhibit 22, TGI Paris 29/09/2016).
faits: Cassiopée claims copyright ownership in Bastille and accuses Méridian Studios of exploiting it without authorization through Quiz Mania (the game 'Carto Royale')

Méridian Studios is the registrant of the trade name Bastille and created the prototype and game design — a documented independent and autonomous creative investment (e-mail […] → Méridian Studios 01/09/2019).
Exhibit 1 (Quiz Mania) presents a game entitled 'Carto Royale' with its own distinct rules, visuals, and mechanics — […] autonomous without slavish reproduction

Exhibit 23 (comparative table of card titles, submitted by the adverse party) constitutes Cassiopée's key exhibit in its attempt to establish formal reproduction — the alleged similarities concern non-protectable themes/ideas
Exhibit 17 (Bastille phrases found online) tends to demonstrate that the disputed formulations belong to the common stock, predating or independent of any original […] by Cassiopée
The TJ Paris (Exhibit 12, December 18, 2020) has already held that originality must be established by the party […] invoking it and that the court may not supply that party's failure to do so.
Exhibit 14 (CA Paris, 10 November 2020) confirms that copyright protection extends only to original elements of expression, not to generic mechanics or themes
analysis.faille: Absence of a formalized, element-by-element comparative analysis demonstrating that each similarity identified by Cassiopée (Exhibit 23) concerns an idea, a theme, or a generic formulation belonging to the public domain, rather than a protectable original expression. Without such an analysis, Farandole 3 could find a prima facie appearance of copying.
analysis.riposte: Argue that: (i) Cassiopée has failed to characterize in what respect the selection or combination of card titles reflects creative choices […] unconstrained by the challenge-game genre […]; (ii) Exhibit 17 demonstrates that the disputed formulations were circulating online prior to any claim by Cassiopée, thereby placing them in the public domain; (iii) Tohu-Bohu features its own distinct visuals, rules, and mechanics (Exhibit 1), precluding any finding of slavish copying; (iv) the court may not supply Cassiopée's failure to characterize originality (TJ Paris, 18/12/2020, Exhibit 12; TGI Paris, 29/09/2016, Exhibit 22).
analysis.pourquoi: Méridian Studios has the email […] (01/09/2019) and the registration of the trade name Bastille to establish its independent creative investment, but the formalized element-by-element comparative analysis — demonstrating that the similarities identified by Cassiopée relate exclusively to non-protectable ideas/themes and not to original expressions — has not yet been constituted as an enforceable exhibit. Exhibit 17 is useful but insufficient without an expert report or a structured bailiff's report.

autorites.title: L122-4 — Code de la propriété intellectuelle
Cass. civ. 1, 11-10.463, 2012-04-05
themes: définition de l'étendue de la protection revendiquée et description des produits argués de contrefaçon
définition de l'étendue de la protection revendiquée et description des […] argués de contrefaçon concurrence deloyale ou illicite
action en réparation du chef de la vente de produits contrefaisants
autorites.summary:
Ayant relevé que l'assignation en contrefaçon de […] de bijoux et en concurrence déloyale du chef de la vente des produits contrefaisants, renvoyait simplement aux photographies […] des […] opposés et souverainement estimé que la seule lecture de la liste des pièces jointes ne permettait pas de déterminer la nature et le nombre des articles incriminés, une cour d'appel a pu retenir, d'une part, que les caractéristiques de chacun des […] revendiqués au titre du droit d'auteur n'étaient pas définies et, d'autre part, que les […] argués de contrefaçon n'étaient ni décrits ni même identifiés. 
Elle en a exactement déduit que le […] avait failli dans l'exposé des […] relatifs à la protection dont il sollicitait le bénéfice et aux agissements qu'il incriminait pour rechercher la responsabilité du défendeur au titre d'actes de contrefaçon et de concurrence déloyale

analysis.acte_referme: Expert report (or adversarial technical memorandum by counsel) conducting a systematic comparison of the Tempo / Tohu-Bohu card titles against the prior online occurrences identified in Exhibit 17, demonstrating that the disputed formulations pre-existed or belong to the common stock — supplemented by a time-stamped bailiff's process-verbal (constat d'huissier) of the prior online sources.
analysis.moyen_adverse: Cassiopée argues that the card titles and challenge structure of Bastille constitute an original selection and combination reflecting the author's personality, and that Tohu-Bohu reproduces the characteristic elements thereof without authorization — relying on Exhibit 23 (comparative table) and on the case law holding that a selection of materials may be original (Cass. civ. 1, 20 mai 1980, n°78-15.475).
analysis.these_adverse: Cassiopée argues, through Exhibit 23 (comparative table of card titles), that Tohu-Bohu reproduces the characteristic selection and wording of the Bastille challenges, which would constitute an original combination reflecting the author's personality within the meaning of L112-1 CPI — relying on case law recognizing that a selection of documents may be original (Cass. civ. 1, 20 mai 1980 [1]). Cassiopée also invokes Exhibits 12 and 22 to recall that the burden of proving originality rests with it, but contends that it has discharged that burden through this comparative table and through Exhibit 2 (Mots du Soir), which illustrates the internal coherence […] of the claimed work. Exhibit 1 (Tohu-Bohu) is presented by Cassiopée not as an […] autonomous work but as a disguised derivative reproducing the essential distinctive elements of Bastille under a different interface.

pieces_adverses.piece: Comparative chart of Tempo / Tohu-Bohu card-title headings (Exhibit 23)
pieces_adverses.contre: Challenge the methodology: the comparison table juxtaposes generic themes and ideas (which are not protectable) without demonstrating the reproduction of any specific original expression; the similarities are explained by the challenge-game genre […], not by copying.

pieces_adverses.piece: Screenshots of Mots du Soir (Exhibit 2) presented as evidence of the original work
pieces_adverses.contre: Challenge the probative value: a screenshot lacking a certified date or legal deposit is insufficient to establish the priority and originality of the claimed work; Cassiopée must produce time-stamped source files

pieces_adverses.piece: Prior TGI/TJ Paris decisions (Exhibits 10, 11, 12) invoked as precedents favorable to Cassiopée
pieces_adverses.contre: Those decisions concern distinct facts and did not definitively resolve the […] of the originality of the Bastille card titles in the context of Tohu-Bohu; Exhibit 12 (TJ Paris 18/12/2020) is, on the contrary, favorable to Méridian Studios on the burden of proof.

pieces_attendues: Element-by-element comparative expert report (Bastille card titles vs. Tohu-Bohu card titles vs. prior online sources)
Time-stamped bailiff's process-verbal (constat d'huissier) of prior online occurrences of the disputed formulations (supplementing Exhibit 17)
E-mail […] → Méridian Studios 01/09/2019 with attached prototype files (establishing the […] autonomous and prior creative authorship of Méridian Studios).
Attestation of certified public accountant Camille Lefèvre 3 regarding the autonomous commercial exploitation of Bastille by Méridian Studios (exhibit already in the record — to be formally filed).

autorites_adverses.title: Cass. civ. 1, 20 mai 1980, n°78-15.475
themes: non présentation sous forme de citations
documents placés à la disposition du public
sélection par l'auteur d'un précédent ouvrage
autorites_adverses.summary: Une Cour d'appel, tout en admettant qu'un ouvrage de vulgarisation, comporte des emprunts aux oeuvres précédentes traitant le même sujet, énonce justement que l'auteur d'un tel ouvrage ne peut pas s'approprier le travail d'autrui en recopiant des passages d'une oeuvre sans les présenter comme une citation, ou en les démarquant. Elle a donc pu estimer qu'il y avait eu contrefaçon, dès lors qu'elle a relevé qu'il existait […] le texte de l'ouvrage […] lui était soumis et une oeuvre précédente des […] extrêmement nombreuses et qu'en dehors des citations mises […] guillemets, il y avait des passages […] avaient été textuellement recopiés.
